academic_requirements.summary: Applicants need at least a three-year bachelor's degree or equivalent. Admission requires 60 CFU/ECTS in the listed business, economics, law, and statistical-mathematical fields, adequate personal preparation, and English at B2 level; applicants with international qualifications must also complete the preliminary evaluation on apply.unive.it.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A bachelor's degree, three-year university diploma, old-system Italian degree, AFAM first-level qualification, or for foreign qualifications at least a three-year bachelor's degree.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: Applicants must have at least 60 CFU/ECTS in the listed business, economics, law, and statistical-mathematical sectors. If they do not meet the curricular requirements, they must complete bachelor's-level single courses before enrolment.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: No separate minimum GPA is stated for all applicants. Applicants with international qualifications must hold a qualification valid for admission and comply with Ca' Foscari's country-specific recognition and document rules.
academic_requirements.committee_or_pre_evaluation: The Teaching Committee evaluates personal preparation through curriculum vitae and studiorum. An interview is required for applicants who do not hold an L-18, L-33, or L-41 degree (or equivalent) with at least 95/110; international-qualification applicants must first pass the Welcome Unit preliminary evaluation on apply.unive.it.

accepted_proofs: A recognised English certification from Ca' Foscari's official list.
A CLA certificate or a certificate from a university language school included in the official recognised-certifications list.
exemption_rules: Completed higher-education studies, or at least one year of college, in a country where English is the medium of instruction.
Obtained a high-school diploma or university qualification after studies taught entirely in English.
Completed at least one full year of high-school exchange at an institution where English was the language of instruction.
Holds one of the specific Italian language-degree qualifications listed by Ca' Foscari for exemption.
language_requirements.assessment_or_upload_stage: Applicants can demonstrate English through a recognised certificate or an exemption category. The programme page states that applicants cannot enrol if they do not meet the language requirement by the enrolment deadline.

rounds.notes: Published on the programme admission page.
Candidates meeting all requirements may receive results before the formal publication date.
Published on the programme admission page.

application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: Standard enrolment runs from July 1, 2026 to September 30, 2026. An additional late period runs from October 1, 2026 to December 18, 2026.
application_deadline_eu.notes: Applicants with international qualifications who fall into the EU/equivalent category must also complete the preliminary evaluation on apply.unive.it.

rounds.application_window: [uncertain] The public apply.unive.it article accessible without selecting a citizenship does not expose the detailed round windows.
rounds.deadline: As of June 5, 2026, Ca' Foscari states that non-EU applicants requiring a study visa can no longer apply for 2026/2027 bachelor's or master's degrees.
rounds.notes: Non-EU applicants who receive a letter of eligibility must then complete Universitaly pre-enrolment and the visa procedure.

application_deadline_non_eu.current_status: Closed for 2026/2027 study-visa applicants as stated on June 5, 2026.
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: Non-EU citizens already legally residing in Italy with a permit valid for enrolment are directed to the EU/equivalent route instead.

required_documents.notes: European applicants may use an ID card.

required_documents.document_name: Valid residence permit or renewal/first-request receipt
required_documents.notes: Required for non-EU applicants already residing in Italy or in the Schengen Area.

required_documents.document_name: Transcript of Records for all undergraduate years, with official translation if needed
required_documents.notes: A provisional transcript is acceptable if the applicant is still attending the final year.
Listed among the mandatory documents for master's applications with an international qualification.
Required in the programme's entry-requirements evaluation request and used for personal-preparation assessment.

required_documents.document_name: Signed self-certification of degree with exams, or signed self-certification of enrolment with exams
required_documents.notes: The programme page specifies that the self-certification must include university, class code, programme name, graduation date and mark, and the exams taken with their sectors and CFU/ECTS weight.

required_documents.document_name: English B2 certificate or exemption evidence
required_documents.notes: The programme page lists it as an additional document useful for the evaluation of personal preparation and requires the language requirement by enrolment.

required_documents.document_name: Bachelor's degree final diploma, with official translation if needed
required_documents.notes: Optional during application if not yet available, but required later to complete enrolment.

required_documents.document_name: CIMEA Statement of Comparability, or Declaration of Value, or ARDI Statement of Correspondence with CIMEA Statement of Verification, or Diploma Supplement
required_documents.notes: Strongly recommended before application and mandatory later to complete enrolment.

required_documents.document_name: Universitaly pre-enrolment submission and study visa evidence
required_documents.notes: Visa-requiring applicants must complete Universitaly pre-enrolment and upload the issued visa to apply.unive.it.

entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: Applicants who do not have an L-18, L-33, or L-41 bachelor's degree (or equivalent) with a final grade of at least 95/110; certain applicants with a master's degree in economics, business, or statistics and at least 95/110 may request exemption.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: June 16, 2026 at 3:00 PM remotely via Zoom; [uncertain] September 2026 and November 2026 sessions were listed without a confirmed date and time on the programme page.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: The interview is passed if the arithmetic mean of the interview score converted to /110 and the bachelor's final grade is at least 88/110.

source_quotes.quote: Candidates must have achieved minimum 60 CFU
A minimum B2-level knowledge of English is mandatory for enrolment.
it is no longer possible for non-EU applicants requiring a visa to apply

uncertainty_notes: The degree permalink and programme interface still display 'Enrolment from 2025/2026', but the admission page explicitly publishes 2026/2027 evaluation-session and enrolment dates.
The public apply.unive.it article for visa-requiring applicants gives closure status, but the detailed citizenship-specific round windows are not exposed in the accessible public text.
The programme page lists September and November 2026 interview sessions without confirming their exact date and time.
